title: [release-1.24] [CI:BUILD] Cirrus: Migrate OSX task to M1
body:
Migrate our OSX build to a M1 instance, since Cirrus is sunsetting Intel-based macOS instances.

Signed-off-by: Ashley Cui <acui@redhat.com>
(cherry picked from commit 498b45770ff9d238f95d772efa2a1fd16d941077)
Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

-->

#### What this PR does / why we need it:
Fixes osx tests in cron run

#### How to verify it

Check the next cron run

Change looks simple enough, but why isn't CI running?
@lsm5 could you repush to see if that triggers CI system?

information_source [Scheduling was delayed due to a concurrency limit on community tasks](https://cirrus-ci.org/faq/#are-there-any-limits)
information_source [Consider upgrading to a $10/month plan to get 2x the limits on any public and private repository](https://github.com/marketplace/cirrus-ci/plan/MDIyOk1hcmtldHBsYWNlTGlzdGluZ1BsYW45OTI=#pricing-and-setup)

This looks new :frowning_face:

[Failure](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5503828255047680?logs=main#L276) looks real:
```
go version
/var/blahblah.sh: line 9: go: command not found
```
@ashley-cui cross-compile task worked here, only diff I see here is PATH doesn't use $GOPATH/bin .
err wait there's also `CGO_ENABLED=0` in make cross.
